claim: MCP acts as a protocol giving AI models 'arms and legs' to interact with APIs and perform tasks on local systems
actionable: Design MCP servers as action executors while the LLM handles reasoning about what actions to take
source_title: Creating Your First MCP Server (With Spring Boot)

claim: Parameter names in @Tool annotations must exactly match the names Claude extracts from user requests for proper argument mapping
actionable: Name tool parameters descriptively (e.g., 'itemName' not 'item') since Claude uses these names to map user input to function arguments
source_title: Creating Your First MCP Server (With Spring Boot)

claim: MCP server communicates via stdio (standard input/output), not HTTP/REST, requiring Spring Boot to disable web server startup
actionable: Set web-application-type=none and disable logging to stdout when building stdio-based MCP servers in Spring Boot
source_title: Creating Your First MCP Server (With Spring Boot)

claim: Tool descriptions are critical for AI models to decide when to use a tool; descriptions must completely describe functionality
actionable: Write detailed tool descriptions that explain inputs, expected outputs, and when the tool should be called
source_title: Creating Your First MCP Server (With Spring Boot)

claim: Claude Desktop can use local MCP servers via configuration in JSON settings file, enabling local-to-remote AI interactions
actionable: Add MCP server startup commands to Claude Desktop's config.json to enable local tool access from the desktop app
source_title: Creating Your First MCP Server (With Spring Boot)
